Transcript
Hearsay
She said that she saw the defendant.
He testified that he had seen him.
She said that she would testify.
He stated that he could identify him.
Luiz, what did the first witness tell the court?
She testified that she had seen our client leave before midnight.
And did the second witness confirm that?
No. He claimed that he had heard nothing, but later he admitted that he had been asleep.
So one of them must have changed the story.
Exactly. In her deposition she said the lights were off; today she asserted that they were on.
Then we argue that the testimony is unreliable.
Agreed. I will request that the transcript be read back to the court.

Good afternoon. This is the court reporter with a summary of this morning's testimony. The first witness stated that she had seen the defendant leave the building at nine o'clock. She added that the side door had been left unlocked. The second witness claimed that he had heard nothing unusual that night. When he was asked about the parked car, he admitted that he had not been paying attention. Counsel for the defense argued that the two accounts could not both be true. The judge replied that the matter would be decided once the transcript had been reviewed. The session was then adjourned until tomorrow.

You are listening to the legal affairs update. In today's hearing, the key witness told the court that she had recognized the defendant immediately. She insisted that her statement had not been changed since the first day. The defense lawyer responded that the witness had given a different account in her earlier deposition. He asked the court why two versions existed. The prosecutor replied that the difference would be explained by a later witness. Reporters were told that a ruling is expected by Friday. We will bring you more as the case develops.
